title: Conjuror's Errand
desc: Deliver supplies to the Conjurors. Try not to look at what they're doing.

steps.text:
Nobody likes delivering to the Conjurors. They live in the deep catacombs — tunnels carved centuries ago and subsequently abandoned by everyone with good sense. The Conjurors moved in because the isolation suits their work, which is the kind of Mugic that makes other Mipedians nervous.

Your cart holds their weekly requisition: salt crystals, distilled water, preserved insects, three jars of indigo dye, and a crate labeled "DO NOT OPEN — ALIVE." The crate vibrates.

The tunnel entrance is marked with symbols you can't read. The air changes immediately — cooler, damper, carrying a faint smell of ozone and something floral you can't place.

options.label: Deliver directly — get in and get out
Take the scenic route through Indigo Grove first

steps.text:
The catacombs twist deeper than you expected. Every junction is marked with symbols that glow faintly — wayfinding Mugic, you assume. The vibrating crate shifts on the cart. You push faster.

The delivery point is a wide chamber lit by floating orbs of pale blue light. A Conjuror meets you — robed, hooded, only their hands visible. Their fingers are stained indigo to the wrist.

"Set it there." They gesture to a stone table. You unload the supplies. The Conjuror inspects each item with unsettling thoroughness, holding the salt crystals up to the light, sniffing the distilled water, pressing an ear to the vibrating crate.

"This one's agitated," they say about the crate. "The journey upset it." They make a soothing sound — a low, humming note that makes your vision blur slightly. The crate stops vibrating.

You do not ask what's in the crate. You do not ask what the indigo dye is for. You do not ask about the sound coming from deeper in the catacombs that sounds like something large breathing.

"Same time next week," the Conjuror says. It's not a question.

You leave faster than you arrived.

Indigo Grove is the surface access point for the catacombs — a cluster of trees whose roots tap into the same underground water that feeds the Conjurors' labs. The trees are stained indigo from root to branch, their leaves a deep purple that bleeds color when it rains.

You stop at the grove to collect fresh indigo sap — a standing request from the Conjurors that saves them a surface trip. The sap runs slow and thick, the color of a bruise. You fill three clay jars.

A young Conjuror apprentice is waiting at the grove's edge, collecting fallen leaves. She's less intimidating than the senior Conjurors — her fingers are only slightly stained, and she actually makes eye contact.

"They'll appreciate the fresh sap," she says. "The stored stuff loses potency after a week." She pauses. "You want to know what it's for?"

You hesitate.

"Ink," she says. "For writing. That's all. Not everything we do is sinister." She grins. "Just most of it."

She helps you carry the supplies down. The delivery goes smoothly — the senior Conjuror accepts the fresh sap with what might be approval, though it's hard to tell through the hood.

The apprentice walks you back to the surface. "Thanks for not asking about the crate," she says. "Everyone asks about the crate. It's better not knowing."

options.label: Agree that not knowing is better
